Full gate green (fmt/clippy/test), feature-matrix builds pass, 81% patch coverage on new code.

title: load-time auto→manual fallback in make_testreport
description: make_testreport(-a) now fetches the QEM-dashboard auto-openQA result at load time and downgrades the working mode to Workflow::Manual when there are no install jobs (or the dashboard is unreachable), matching upstream AutoOBSUpdateID.make_testreport (python-mtui/mtui/types/updateid.py:298-327). Previously it hard-coded Workflow::Auto with a stale TODO(mtui-rs-m1w/zs4/plt) — those beads wired enrichment into export/reload_openqa/set_workflow but never the load path, so -a always stayed 'auto' regardless of openQA coverage (wrong prompt/mode). Autoconnect (answer B, upstream parity): the reference-host connect is deferred ONLY on the manual-downgrade path; the auto happy-path and kernel never autoconnect on load. Files: crates/mtui-testreport/src/lifecycle.rs (+ tests). Full gate green. Plan: plans/load-time-auto-manual-fallback.md
close_reason: Implemented load-time auto→manual fallback in make_testreport: builds QemIncident + runs DashboardAutoOpenQA for -a, downgrades to Workflow::Manual when no install jobs / dashboard unreachable, stores openqa.auto result. Autoconnect gated on the manual-downgrade path only (answer B, upstream parity). Docs updated (removed no-op/TODO narration). 3 wiremock lifecycle tests (downgrade+pending, stays-auto no-connect, autoconnect=false suppresses) + existing auto/SLFO tests repointed at mock dashboards. Full workspace gate green: fmt --check, clippy -D warnings, test --workspace.

title: add_host: verify product drift against refhosts on connect
description: Upstream add_target/connect_target call _verify_target_products(target) after connect (python-mtui/mtui/test_reports/testreport.py:474,547,763): compare the host's detected System against its refhosts.yml row and record/log product_warnings for base/arch/addon drift. Rust Session::connect_and_add_hosts (crates/mtui-core/src/session.rs:379) never does this, so add_host/autoconnect emit no drift warning. Primitives exist in Rust (parse_system runs in Target::connect; the refhosts store is built in resolve_testplatform_hosts). Wire a shared post-connect verification into connect_and_add_hosts so both autoconnect and add_host get it, mirroring upstream connect_targets. See plans/add_host-parity-findings.md #1.
close_reason: Wired post-connect product-drift verification into the shared connect_and_add_hosts seam: build_refhosts_store (shared with resolve_testplatform_hosts) + verify_target_products (mtui_datasources::compare, qa ignored) + apply_product_warnings (surfaces yellow display block to user, records product_warnings, WARN-logs, clears stale entries). Best-effort; host always kept. Covers autoconnect and add_host (with/without -t). 6 new unit tests. Full gate green.

title: add_host: PI-autolock freshly added hosts when lock_comment is set
description: Upstream add_target calls _autolock_new_target(target) (testreport.py:435,564,764): when a PI assignment is active (lock_comment set) the newly connected host is locked with that comment (a host already locked by someone else is left as-is, TargetLockedError suppressed). Rust connect_and_add_hosts does not consult lock_comment. Wireable now: TestReportBase.lock_comment exists (crates/mtui-testreport/src/testreport.rs:84, set by the PI-assign apicall command apicall.rs:80) and Target::lock(comment) exists (mtui-hosts target/mod.rs:472). Add the autolock after a successful connect in connect_and_add_hosts, suppressing an already-locked error. See plans/add_host-parity-findings.md #2.
close_reason: Wired PI-autolock into shared connect_and_add_hosts seam (Session::autolock_target): freshly connected hosts are locked with the active report's lock_comment when a PI assignment is active, mirroring upstream _autolock_new_target; foreign TargetLocked suppressed, other errors logged but non-fatal. 3 unit tests (lock set, empty no-op, foreign-lock suppression). Full workspace gate green.

title: add_host -t: warn+skip already-connected hosts (add_target parity)
description: Upstream add_target warns 'already connected to X, skipping.' and returns early when hostname in self.targets (testreport.py:744-748). Rust add_named_hosts→connect_and_add_hosts (session.rs:441,379) builds+connects unconditionally for explicit -t; the no--t path already dedups via 'already'. Target::connect short-circuits the re-dial (no corruption) but re-runs parse_system/query_versions and emits no warning. Add a membership pre-check + warning for the -t path. Minor. See plans/add_host-parity-findings.md #3.
